title: God Does Like Ugly - The GRAMMYs 2026 Best Rap Album Nomination
text: New Industry Focus reports on JID's God Does Like Ugly as a 2026 Grammy Best Rap Album nominee. JID's fourth studio album God Does Like Ugly follows his acclaimed 2022 project The Forever Story and marks a new creative and personal chapter for the Atlanta rapper. The album features high-profile collaborations with Clipse, Vince Staples, Earthgang, and Ciara. Rolling Stone praised the album's meticulous narrative cohesion and JID's ability to balance the promised salvation of Christianity with the grit of Atlanta's reality. JID represents a category of rapper that prioritizes technical skill and lyrical complexity over commercial convenience. While JID did not win Best Rap Album, his nomination reflected the Recording Academy's continued recognition of technically excellent hip-hop. The album showcases JID's growth as an artist and his commitment to pushing the boundaries of traditional hip-hop within a commercial framework.
